name: Belmonte de Campos from Villanueva de San Mancio — circular by GR89 the river Anguijon
name_original: Belmonte de Campos desde Villanueva de San Mancio — circular por GR89 el río Anguijon
region_intro: Hiking around Villanueva De San Mancio offers exploration of a landscape shaped by historical engineering and agricultural plains in the Valladolid province. The region is characterized by the historic Canal de Castilla, providing flat trails along its banks, and the Río Sequillo, which offers peaceful riverside walks. While generally flat, the Páramo de los Torozos to the south introduces some elevation, complementing the extensive cereal fields that define the area. These features create a varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Villanueva De San Mancio The most popular hiking route is Madrid Way of Saint James, a 11.2 miles (18.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate path forms part of a historic pilgrimage route. Another top favourite among local hikers is Palacios de Campos from Montealegre — circular via the Camino del Tierra de Campos, a moderate 6.8 miles (11.0 km) path. This route traverses the agricultural landscapes of the Tierra de Campos. Local hikers also love the Belmonte de Campos from Villanueva de San Mancio — circular by GR89 the river Anguijon, a 6.6 miles (10.5 km) trail leading through riverside paths, often completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes. Hiking around Villanueva De San Mancio is defined by the Canal de Castilla, the Río Sequillo, and vast agricultural fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, primarily featuring moderate routes. The routes in Villanueva De San Mancio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 29 hikers have used komoot to explore Villanueva De San Mancio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails around Villanueva de San Mancio?
A: The hiking trails around Villanueva de San Mancio are primarily characterized by flat paths along the historic Canal de Castilla and peaceful riverside walks along the Río Sequillo. While generally flat, the &lt;p&gt;Páramo de los Torozos&lt;/p&gt; to the south offers some gentle elevation changes. You'll also encounter extensive cereal fields, creating picturesque backdrops, especially in spring.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available near Villanueva de San Mancio?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For example, you can explore the agricultural landscapes on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1195880956/palacios-de-campos-from-montealegre-circular-via-the-camino-del-tierra-de-campos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palacios de Campos from Montealegre — circular via the Camino del Tierra de Campos&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 11 km long. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1198279248/belmonte-de-campos-from-villanueva-de-san-mancio-circular-by-gr89-the-river-anguijon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmonte de Campos from Villanueva de San Mancio — circular by GR89 the river Anguijon&lt;/a&gt;, offering riverside paths over 10.5 km.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I see while hiking in the area?
A: The region is rich in historical engineering and cultural sites. Along the Canal de Castilla, you can visit the impressive 18th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5814689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lock 7 and Old Flour Factory&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1789312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montealegre de Campos Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a 13th-century fortification, and the Herrerian-style &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4590982" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa Cruz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Villanueva de San Mancio suitable for families?
A: Many of the trails, particularly those along the Canal de Castilla and Río Sequillo, are relatively flat and accessible, making them suitable for families. The moderate difficulty of most routes means they can be enjoyed by various fitness levels. For a pleasant walk, consider the path following the Sequillo River from Medina de Rioseco to Villanueva de San Mancio.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Villanueva de San Mancio?
A: Yes, the natural landscapes and open trails along the Canal de Castilla and Río Sequillo are generally dog-friendly. Always ensure your dog is under control, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife. It's advisable to carry water for your pet, particularly during warmer months, as shaded areas can be limited.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Villanueva de San Mancio?
A: Spring is an excellent time to visit, as the extensive cereal fields are revitalized by moderate rainfall, offering vibrant green landscapes. Autumn also provides pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if hiking during this season.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options in the region?
A: Yes, the area includes a section of a historic pilgrimage route. You can hike part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085008246/madrid-way-of-saint-james" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Madrid Way of Saint James&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 18 km trail offering a longer-distance experience through the local landscape.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails near Villanueva de San Mancio?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Villanueva de San Mancio and nearby towns like Medina de Rioseco. Many trailheads along the Canal de Castilla or near village centers offer convenient spots to leave your vehicle. Specific parking details may vary by starting point, so checking local maps or komoot route details for suggested parking is recommended.

Q: What amenities are available near the hiking routes, such as cafes or accommodation?
A: Villanueva de San Mancio and nearby towns like Medina de Rioseco offer basic amenities. You can find local cafes and restaurants for refreshments. For accommodation, there are rural houses and guesthouses that integrate traditional Castilian architecture with modern comforts, often providing panoramic views of the surrounding fields and the Canal de Castilla.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Villanueva de San Mancio?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the tranquil natural landscapes, the historical significance of the Canal de Castilla, and the peaceful riverside walks. The generally flat terrain also makes it enjoyable for a wide range of hikers.

Q: Are there any trails that specifically highlight the Canal de Castilla?
A: Absolutely. The Canal de Castilla is a central feature for hiking in the region. You can enjoy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158880" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla Dock – Canal de Castilla loop from Medina de Rioseco&lt;/a&gt;, which is an 8.4 km moderate hike directly along its banks, allowing you to experience this historic waterway up close.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in Villanueva de San Mancio?
A: There are at least 5 distinct hiking routes around Villanueva de San Mancio, offering various distances and experiences. All currently listed routes are rated as moderate, providing accessible options for most hikers.
tips: Lock No. 7 of the Campos branch of the Canal de Castilla, located in Tamariz de Campos. Like all the canal's locks, its purpose was to allow barges to overcome uneven terrain, facilitating the transport of goods, especially grain and flour.

Magnificent tower built on a small hill on the outskirts of the small town of Belmonte de Campos next to the Anguijón river. If you are touring the GR-89 promenade, you should make it one of your stops.

waypoints.name: Lock No. 7 of the Canal de Castilla (Tamariz de Campos)
